title: dubautia imbricata subsp. imbricata
intro: Dubautia imbricata subsp. imbricata, also known as the Hawaiian hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating wounds. The herbal preparations of Dubautia imbricata subsp. imbricata include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are often used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
